fixtures.reasoning: A successful payment intent from Stripe is a customer-facing event that warrants sending a payment confirmation email to the receipt email address provided.
Payment failures are operational events requiring immediate team attention to monitor fraud patterns and system issues, making Slack the best channel for real-time alerting.
A new Stripe customer account was created, which is a customer-facing event that warrants a welcome email to the customer with their account details.
A charge refund is a customer-facing financial event that requires direct notification to the customer via email to confirm the refund has been processed.
This is a successful payment event that should trigger a customer-facing confirmation email to the invoice payer.
A subscription creation is a customer-facing event that warrants confirmation communication, making send_email the best action to notify the customer of their new subscription.
A subscription cancellation is a significant customer-facing event that warrants an email notification to confirm the cancellation and provide subscription details.

This is a Stripe charge dispute that requires immediate operational attention since it has status 'needs_response' with a deadline, making it an urgent internal event best suited for Slack notification to the payments team.
This is a successful payment completion event that should trigger a customer-facing confirmation email to the buyer.
Payment method attachment is an internal system event that should be audited for compliance and record-keeping, but doesn't require immediate operational alerts or customer notifications.
GitHub push events to main branch are operational events best handled by notifying the engineering team via Slack for visibility and coordination.
Pull request opened events are operational notifications best delivered to Slack for team visibility and real-time collaboration.
This is a merged pull request event which is a typical operational notification best suited for Slack to keep the development team informed of code integration.
This is a bug report on an operational GitHub repository that requires immediate engineering attention, making a Slack notification the appropriate action for real-time team visibility.
GitHub issue closures are operational team events that warrant real-time Slack notification to keep the development team informed of project progress.
GitHub star events are informational metrics that don't require immediate operational alerts or customer notifications, making logging the most appropriate action for audit and analytics purposes.

This is a repository fork event that should be logged for auditing and tracking purposes, as it doesn't require immediate operational action like a failed payment or PR review, nor is it a customer-facing transaction.
GitHub release published events are operational announcements best delivered to Slack for team visibility and coordination.
GitHub deployment events are operational in nature and require immediate team awareness; Slack notification is the best choice for alerting the ops/dev team about production deployments.
GitHub check run failures are operational events that require immediate team notification, making Slack the appropriate choice for alerting developers about the failing CI tests.

This is a general Slack conversation asking about PR review status, not an operational event (PR/deploy/payment failure) or customer-facing event, so logging is the appropriate safe default action for audit purposes.
This is a Slack app mention requesting operational information about webhook queue depth, which requires an immediate response back to the same Slack channel where the request was made.
A reaction_added event is a user interaction that should be recorded for auditing purposes, but does not require immediate operational notification or customer-facing communication.
Channel creation is an internal infrastructure event that should be recorded for audit trails and historical tracking, not an operational alert requiring immediate notification.

Member joining a Slack channel is an internal organizational event that should be recorded for auditing and historical purposes, but does not require immediate operational notifications or customer-facing communications.
File sharing is an informational event that requires audit tracking but doesn't need immediate notification to users or external channels.

This is an internal Slack activity (message pinned) that should be recorded for audit purposes without requiring immediate notification, as it's not an operational event like a deployment or PR, nor a customer-facing event.

This is a Slack workflow_step_execute event that explicitly specifies a target channel (C_reports), making send_slack_message the natural action for delivering the daily report notification to that channel.
A new team member has joined, which is an operational/HR event best handled by notifying the Slack channel so the team is aware of the new hire.

A link_shared event from Slack is informational metadata that doesn't require immediate operational notification or customer-facing communication, making logging the most appropriate action for audit and tracking purposes.
